canonicalKinds.name: Command Injection
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input reaches a command execution sink without proper validation or allowlisting
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often execute commands as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled input reaches the execution sink without a guard such as a command allowlist.

canonicalKinds.name: Path Traversal
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input controls file paths without workspace-root enforcement or path canonicalization
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often read and write files as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled paths escape the intended workspace root.

canonicalKinds.name: Server-Side Request Forgery
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input controls outbound request targets without host or scheme restrictions
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often make outbound network requests as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled URLs reach the request sink without a host allowlist or scheme restriction.
